Package io.cloudstub.core.spi

Interface StubRequest

public interface StubRequest
Read-only view of an incoming HTTP request, handed to a StubHandler at request time.

This is the only request abstraction in CloudStub's public SPI. It exposes just what a module needs to translate an AWS API call into state-store operations — method, path, body, headers, query parameters, and JSON body fields — without leaking any WireMock, servlet, or JSON-library type. The implementation lives in cloudstub-core's internal package and adapts the underlying networking driver.

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    String
    body()
    The raw request body as a string, or an empty string if there is none.
    String
    header(String name)
    The value of the named request header, case-insensitively, or null if absent.
    String
    jsonField(String path)
    Reads a scalar field from the JSON request body, addressed by a dotted path, so JSON-protocol modules (SQS, DynamoDB, Lambda, …) never have to write their own parser.

    method()
    The HTTP method of the request.
    String

  • Method Details

    • method

      HttpMethod method()
      The HTTP method of the request.

    • path

      String path()
      The request path (without query string), e.g. "/my-bucket/my-key".

    • body

      String body()
      The raw request body as a string, or an empty string if there is none.

    • header

      String header(String name)
      The value of the named request header, case-insensitively, or null if absent.
      Parameters:
      name - header name, e.g. "X-Amz-Target"

    • queryParam

      String queryParam(String name)
      The value of the named query-string parameter, or null if absent. When a parameter is repeated, this returns its first value; use queryParamValues(java.lang.String) to read them all.
      Parameters:
      name - query parameter name

    • queryParamValues

      default List<String> queryParamValues(String name)
      All values of the named query-string parameter, in request order, for a parameter that may repeat (e.g. ?tagKeys=a&tagKeys=b). Returns an empty list if the parameter is absent.
      Parameters:
      name - query parameter name
      Returns:
      the values, or an empty list; never null

    • jsonField

      String jsonField(String path)
      Reads a scalar field from the JSON request body, addressed by a dotted path, so JSON-protocol modules (SQS, DynamoDB, Lambda, …) never have to write their own parser. The body is parsed once per request.

      The path is a sequence of segments separated by '.'; a leading "$." is tolerated for familiarity with JSONPath. A numeric segment indexes into a JSON array. Examples: "QueueName", "Item.id.S", "Records.0.eventName", "$.MessageBody". String values are returned unescaped; numbers and booleans are returned in their JSON textual form (e.g. "10", "true").

      Returns null if the body is empty or not valid JSON, the path is empty or absent, or the value at the path is JSON null or a non-scalar (object/array). It never throws on malformed input — a bad body yields null, not an exception out of the handler.

      Parameters:
      path - dotted path to a scalar field, e.g. "QueueName"
      Returns:
      the field value as a string, or null
